minutes_briefs.title: BC Firearm Violence Prevention Act takes effect October 1, 2026
minutes_briefs.summary: British Columbia's new Firearm Violence Prevention Act will come into force on October 1, 2026. This law creates new rules for owning, storing, and using firearms in the province. The old Firearm (Authorization) Regulation is being repealed (cancelled) at the same time. Some parts of the new law are delayed, including sections about certain licence conditions and some reporting requirements.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the new Firearm Violence Prevention Act requirements with a lawyer if your business involves firearms sales, storage, or training.

key_dates.label: BC Firearm Violence Prevention Act takes effect

minutes_briefs.source_body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 63/2026
Made under: Firearm Act
Published in: No. 8 – B.C. Regs. 62 to 76, May 5, 2026
Deposit date: April 27, 2026

B.C. Reg. 63/2026, deposited April 27, 2026, under theFIREARM ACT[section 10] andFIREARM VIOLENCE PREVENTION ACT[section 102]. Order in Council 156/2026, approved and ordered April 27, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Lieutenant Governor, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that, effective October 1, 2026, (a)	theFirearm Violence Prevention Act, S.B.C. 2021, c. 7, is brought into force, except the following: (i)	sections 5 (2), 15 and 16 (c); (iv)	sections 65 (1) (i) and 66 (1) (b), (c), (d), (e), (f) and (g), (4) and (5); (v)	sections 78 (2), 81 (2) (c), 83, 84, 89 (b) and 93, and (b)	the Firearm (Authorization) Regulation, B.C. Reg. 373/80, is repealed. — N. KRIEGER,Minister of Public Safety and Solicitor General; N. SHARMA,Presiding Member of the Executive Council.
